sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.94 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: By definition two elements$g_1$and$g_2$of a free metabelian group$F$with basis$\{x_1, , x_n\}$have the same distribution if the equations$g_1(x_1, , x_n) = g$and$g_2(x_1, , x_n) = g$have the same number of solutions in any finite metabelian group$G$for every$g G$. Is it true that elements$g_1$and$g_2$have the same distribution if and only if they are conjugate by some automorphism of$F?$

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
